comments.body: @vrothberg Yes, I want to talk to @baude about setting up buildah to use additional stores like podman does, then this would be easier to run tests on.

Looks like an images test needs some tweaking.  Code changes LGTM otherwise.

```
not ok 227 images json test
# (from function `expect_line_count' in file ./helpers.bash, line 228,
#  in test file ./images.bats, line 93)
#   `expect_line_count 14' failed
```
LGTM, assuming happy tests.  Looks like Red Hat CI has fallen off the rails again.  I'm sure unrelated.
@cevich How can we move to the /lgtm way of merging?

Approved by: @TomSweeneyRedHat
Pushing 555b5a5015147cef832d843e87dddbcad4d43350 to master...

> @cevich How can we move to the /lgtm way of merging?

I'm having trouble imagining a way that isn't heavily time-consuming in some respect.

*  Adding this repo. to the OpenShift Prow system is one option (what libpod does), it kind of works, but has some complex issues.
* The traditional way of doing this is to have a small VM somewhere, running a bot that periodically monitors PRs.  I know how to do this, it's not hard, just needs time.
* A possibly easier way, could be to hook into the new Github-Actions system (in beta, but mostly stable).  I have no idea how to do this, would need to spend time researching.

The last two options could be applied to other repositories, so only the initial cost is high.
